_comment: Connector-specific edits a cli-printing-press reprint must NOT clobber. check_handfixes.py asserts every marker below is still present (and banned anti-patterns absent) and fails CI if a reprint dropped one. Provenance: issue #115 (reporter @AvlCompCo), fixed 2026-06-16. The fix is documentation + error-string only; no new flag/command. N-able removed the per-user 'API access' checkbox and moved to dedicated API Users; API Users still authenticate via the JSON-RPC Login method (partner=customer name, username=API-user login, password=API token -> visa), so COVE_PARTNER is REQUIRED for them. There is no bearer/header auth in the Cove API; the API token is the Login password, not a visa.

handfixes.summary: Credential error hints (data 2100 / missing creds) name COVE_PARTNER as required for API Users
handfixes.why: Cove's data-2100 'Unknown partner/username' is most often an empty COVE_PARTNER for an API User scoped to a customer. The original hint pointed at the removed per-user 'API access' checkbox, which no longer exists in backup.management. Re-derived from N-able's API User authorization docs (issue #115).

asserts.contains: an empty COVE_PARTNER is the usual cause

asserts.not_contains: that the user has API access enabled

asserts.contains: COVE_PARTNER (the customer the API user was created for)

handfixes.summary: `auth` command long help describes the API User flow (Users > API Users; token is the password, not a visa)
handfixes.why: The generated auth help previously taught the removed 'backup.management user with API access enabled' flow. A reprint regenerates this novel command as a stub and would drop the API-User guidance.

asserts.not_contains: a backup.management user with API access

handfixes.summary: SKILL/README/guide/page auth sections teach the API User flow with COVE_PARTNER required
handfixes.why: The authored auth prose described the removed per-user 'API access' toggle and marked COVE_PARTNER optional. Issue #115 reporter followed it and could not authenticate. A full re-onboard regenerates these surfaces; keep the API-User guidance.

asserts.contains: COVE_PARTNER is required for API Users
